claims.claim_text: The US does not generally operate a Colombia-style exchange-control regime, but transfers remain subject to sanctions, AML/BSA, tax, and reporting rules.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: reworded to avoid overstating; add a Treasury/OFAC overview source before verifying.

claims.claim_text: Bitcoin is legal to own; 2025 federal posture moved toward institutional integration; the defining tension for holders is reporting, not legality.
US citizens and green-card holders are taxed on worldwide income regardless of where they live.
Bitcoin is treated as property; disposals are taxable events reported on Form 8949 and Schedule D.
Bitcoin is classified as property (not currency) for US federal income tax.
A Bitcoin-to-stablecoin trade is generally treated as an exchange/disposition of digital-asset property and may create gain or loss.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: IRS treats digital-asset-for-digital-asset exchanges as reportable dispositions; cleared with cautious wording.

claims.claim_text: No Lightning-specific IRS tax guidance was found in this research pass.
claims.reviewer_notes: Publish only as an open question for tax counsel.

claims.claim_text: Form 1099-DA broker reporting: gross proceeds for transactions on/after Jan 1 2025; adjusted basis for certain covered transactions on/after Jan 1 2026; backup-withholding relief extended to Jan 1 2027 (Notice 2025-33).
The US is not a CRS participating jurisdiction and relies on FATCA instead.
claims.reviewer_notes: Sourced to the OECD CRS-by-jurisdiction list (US absent) plus the FATCA structure. Confirm OECD URL resolves at next review.

claims.claim_text: The US has implemented domestic digital-asset broker reporting through Form 1099-DA; its CARF participation/coordination status should be separately confirmed.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: do NOT say US chose 1099-DA 'instead of CARF' without a Treasury/OECD source; reworded.

claims.claim_text: A foreign account holding only virtual currency is currently not FBAR-reportable; FinCEN noticed intent to amend the rules to include virtual currency; the amendment is not finalized as of this review.
claims.reviewer_notes: Time-sensitive; re-verify finalization status each quarter.

claims.claim_text: Self-custody is legal; FinCEN guidance FIN-2019-G001 treats non-transmitting unhosted-wallet software providers as not money transmitters; the 2020 unhosted-wallet proposal was withdrawn in 2024.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary self-custody-treatment claim sourced to FIN-2019-G001 (Tier 1). The 2024 withdrawal of the 2020 proposal rests on Tier-3 context; keep that sub-point cautious and re-verify the withdrawal citation.

claims.claim_text: Spot Bitcoin ETPs trade on US national exchanges (SEC approved listing/trading Jan 10, 2024).
claims.reviewer_notes: Sourced to SEC approval statement, Release No. 34-99306.

claims.claim_text: The GENIUS Act, enacted 18 July 2025 (Public Law 119-27), created a federal US payment-stablecoin framework; implementation details depend on rulemaking and effective-date provisions.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: corrected from 'advanced' to enacted (PL 119-27, 18 Jul 2025).

claims.claim_text: Most US states generally allow broad testamentary freedom, subject to spouse/elective-share and other state-law limits; Louisiana has a limited forced-heirship regime.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: reworded; add a Tier-1/2 source (Uniform Law Commission/state statutes) before verifying.

claims.claim_text: The 2026 federal lifetime estate/gift exemption is $15,000,000 per individual (up from $13.99M in 2025); several states levy their own estate or inheritance taxes with lower thresholds.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed against IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32 (Tier 1). State-level estate/inheritance taxes still vary; verify per state when a user's state is known.

claims.claim_text: The 2026 annual gift-tax exclusion is $19,000 per recipient.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed against IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32 (Tier 1).

claims.claim_text: A US executor or trustee can hold full legal authority and still be unable to move self-custodied Bitcoin without the keys.
claims.reviewer_notes: Definitional (authority vs technical control). Central teaching point.

claims.claim_text: Section 877A imposes a deemed sale of worldwide property on a 'covered expatriate'; covered if net worth >= $2,000,000, or 5-year average annual net income tax exceeds the indexed threshold ($206,000 for 2025), or failure to certify 5 years of compliance on Form 8854; a gain exclusion applied ($890,000 for 2025).
claims.reviewer_notes: Annual thresholds are inflation-indexed; confirm the current-year figures each filing season.

claims.claim_text: The substantial-presence test can make a non-citizen a US tax resident based on day counts.
Legal authority does not equal key access; this is the dominant inheritance planning gap for self-custodied Bitcoin.
Colombia runs an exchange-control regime; crypto is not foreign currency and cannot be channeled through the FX market, so crypto-funded inflows are not registrable.
claims.reviewer_notes: Agent-sourced from Banco de la República; reviewer to re-open the primary.

claims.claim_text: DIAN treats cryptoassets as intangible assets forming part of patrimonio (Oficio 30470/2019).
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (DIAN normograma).

claims.claim_text: Crypto gains may be treated differently depending on holding period, taxpayer profile, and whether the activity is habitual; local tax counsel should confirm the exact treatment.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: no primary DIAN source for the exact >2yr/15% vs ordinary formulation for Bitcoin; softened.

claims.claim_text: Colombia has a permanent individual wealth tax (Law 2277-style reform architecture), generally keyed to UVT thresholds; use 72,000 UVT unless a newer primary source proves otherwise. The reported ~40,000 UVT figure is NOT used absent a current legislative source.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: cleared for existence + 72,000 UVT threshold; 40,000 UVT figure dropped pending a primary source.

claims.claim_text: No deemed-disposal exit tax confirmed; not affirmatively verified.
SFC does not regulate/supervise/endorse crypto; no exchange licensing regime; supervised banks not authorized to intermediate crypto.
claims.reviewer_notes: Agent-sourced from SFC; reviewer to re-open.

claims.claim_text: Tax residents with foreign assets > 2,000 UVT must file the foreign-asset declaration (Form 160); foreign-held Bitcoin counts.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (DIAN; threshold 2,000 UVT).

claims.claim_text: DIAN Resolución 000240/2025 requires crypto-asset service providers to report user/transaction data from TY2026 (CARF-aligned).
claims.reviewer_notes: Corroborated by Holland & Knight; confirm the DIAN resolution text.

claims.claim_text: Forced heirship: since Ley 1934/2018, legítima rigurosa (half) reserved for forced heirs; the other half freely disposable (mejoras + libre disposición merged). Old 50/25/25 is pre-2018.
claims.reviewer_notes: Corrected + verified 2026-06-16 against Ley 1934/2018; prior text was the pre-2018 regime.

claims.claim_text: Inheritance/donation tax treatment (commonly described as ganancia ocasional) should be confirmed against DIAN/Congress primary text before publishing.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: primary DIAN/Congress source not retrieved; do not publish as confirmed.

claims.claim_text: Panama removed from the FATF grey list 27 Oct 2023 and from the EU AML list.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (FATF/KPMG/Basel/MEF).

claims.claim_text: Territorial taxation: only Panama-source income taxed; foreign-source exempt even if remitted. Residence via >183 days or center of economic interest.
claims.reviewer_notes: Core principle confirmed across many sources 2026-06-16; source-vs-Panama-source edge cases remain fact-specific.

claims.claim_text: No published DGI crypto tax guidance found; foreign-source crypto gains prima facie outside the territorial net but not confirmed by a DGI ruling.
No wealth tax; no inheritance, estate, or gift tax (inheritance tax abolished 26 Dec 2002).
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 across PwC + multiple independent sources.

claims.claim_text: Panama's 2022 crypto law was declared unconstitutional (July 2023); no general crypto law is in force; crypto is legal but unregulated.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (Central Banking + Orbitax).

claims.claim_text: CARF commitment status is in conflict: one OECD-derived reading says not committed; a 2025 source lists Panama as committed for 2027/2028/2029.
claims.reviewer_notes: CONFLICT found 2026-06-16 (not-committed vs committed-2027/2028/2029); downgraded High->Low and moved to open questions (not rendered as a statement). Confirm against the live OECD CARF commitments list.

claims.claim_text: Freedom of testation with a valid will (no reserved portion); intestate succession distributes equal shares to children/descendants (per stirpes), then ascendants, with the spouse.
claims.reviewer_notes: Reconciled + confirmed 2026-06-16 (IBA + Lexology + Pardini): testate freedom vs intestate equal-share.

claims.claim_text: Panama private-interest foundations (Law 25 of 1995) are commonly used in succession planning, but whether they place assets outside forced-heirship claims depends on the settlor's/heirs' domicile, situs, marital property, fraudulent-transfer and conflict-of-laws rules and must be confirmed by counsel.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: 'assets outside forced heirship' is a legal conclusion; reworded to avoid overstatement.

claims.claim_text: Qualified Investor Visa: real estate US$300,000 until 15 Oct 2026 then US$500,000; securities US$500,000; deposit US$750,000.
claims.reviewer_notes: Threshold + Oct-2026 reversion confirmed across many sources 2026-06-16; confirm against the live immigration decree at application.

claims.claim_text: Bitcoin's legal-tender/mandatory-acceptance status was repealed 29 Jan 2025 (now voluntary); taxes no longer payable in BTC. IMF-conditioned.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (IMF + multiple). Corrects the stale 2021 legal-tender narrative.

claims.claim_text: Reported Bitcoin capital-gains exemption survived the Jan 2025 amendments, but not confirmed against a Tier-1 Hacienda source.
LEAD (Digital Assets Issuance Law, 2023) regulates issuers/providers; CNAD licenses and supervises.
Reported among non-committed CARF jurisdictions with weak/absent CRS; but the CARF committed list is growing — re-verify.
claims.reviewer_notes: Downgraded Medium->Low 2026-06-16; re-confirm against live OECD CARF list.

claims.claim_text: Intestate succession (Código Civil) calls children, parents, and spouse by order, dividing equally within each order.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: split per reviewer. Intestate order is confirmable against the Código Civil (attach exact article to verify). Testate reserve is sv-est-002.

claims.claim_text: Whether El Salvador retains a testate forced-heirship reserve (legítima) is CONFLICTED across sources (some say absolute testamentary freedom, others reference forced heirs); unresolved.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: split from sv-est-001; testate reserve unresolved, kept off public rendering.

claims.claim_text: Default marital regime is sociedad conyugal (community of marital gains) absent capitulaciones; gains shared on dissolution, separate from the succession.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (Ministerio de Justicia).

claims.claim_text: Default matrimonial regime is participation in acquisitions; acquired-property surplus shared on dissolution; settlement precedes succession.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (ch.ch).

claims.claim_text: No community-property regime; Family Law Act 1975 divides property on separation by contributions and future needs, not a fixed share.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (AIFS; Family Law Act 1975).

claims.claim_text: Bitcoin is a CGT asset (property); disposal (sell, gift, trade, spend) is a CGT event.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (ATO).

claims.claim_text: Individuals may apply a 50% CGT discount on gains where the asset is held >= 12 months.
Personal-use-asset gain disregarded only if acquisition cost <= AUD 10,000 and mainly for personal use; investment holdings do not qualify.
Digital Assets Framework became law (Royal Assent Apr 2026); Digital Asset Platform / Tokenised Custody Platform under AFSL/ASIC; obligations commence Apr 2027.
CRS participant since 2015; committed to CARF (first crypto exchange ~2028); FATF travel rule for VA transfers from 1 July 2026.
Ceasing Australian tax residency triggers CGT event I1: deemed disposal of crypto at market value (election to defer available); rates up to ~47% with 50% discount if eligible.
claims.reviewer_notes: Primary-confirmed 2026-06-16 (ATO; s104-160 ITAA 1997).

claims.claim_text: Bitcoin may be held via an SMSF only under super rules: sole-purpose test, separation of assets, wallet in the fund's name, annual audit, no acquisition from a related party.
Crypto-business de-banking is a recognised, documented problem (CFR 2022 policy paper).

Testamentary freedom qualified by family-provision law in each state/territory; eligible persons (spouse, ex-spouse, de facto, child, dependent, close personal relationship) may seek provision. NSW Succession Act 2006 s57/s60; QLD/VIC/SA have own Acts.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16; NSW s57/s60 + per-state Acts named.

claims.claim_text: Gains on private movable assets (incl. Bitcoin) are income-tax exempt at all three levels (DBG art. 16(3)).
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (FTA + multiple).

claims.claim_text: Private-investor exemption lost if deemed a professional securities trader; FTA Circular 36 five safe-harbour criteria (6-mo holding, <=5x turnover, gains not replacing income, gains <50% of income, derivatives only to hedge).
claims.reviewer_notes: Five criteria confirmed 2026-06-16; Medium because reclassification is facts-and-circumstances.

claims.claim_text: Privately held Bitcoin is subject to cantonal/communal net-wealth tax (there is no federal wealth tax); rates vary by canton/municipality.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: cleared for cantonal/communal wealth taxation. Valuation-rate subclaim split out to ch-tax-005.

claims.claim_text: The exact FTA year-end valuation rate used to value Bitcoin for net-wealth tax is a separate point not confirmed against an FTA source this pass.
claims.reviewer_notes: Split from ch-tax-003 per human review 2026-06-16; attach the FTA year-end rate-list source.

claims.claim_text: DLT Act in force 1 Aug 2021 created ledger-based securities and the FINMA DLT trading-facility license (first granted Mar 2025).
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (FINMA + DLT Act date).

claims.claim_text: Switzerland participates in CRS (in force 2017; first exchange autumn 2018; 100+ partners).
claims.reviewer_notes: First-exchange-2018 confirmed 2026-06-16.

claims.claim_text: Switzerland is moving toward CARF implementation; the first-exchange timing should be checked against current Swiss Federal Council / FDF materials.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: date kept fluid; confirm against Swiss federal primary.

claims.claim_text: 2023 reform (in force 1 Jan 2023) cut descendants' reserved portion 3/4 -> 1/2 of the intestate share; parents' compulsory portion abolished; spouse 1/2 unchanged.
claims.reviewer_notes: Confirmed 2026-06-16 (3/4->1/2 of statutory share).

claims.claim_text: No federal inheritance/gift tax; cantonal: spouses/registered partners exempt in all cantons; direct descendants exempt in virtually all cantons except Appenzell Innerrhoden (CHF 300k/child then ~1%), Vaud, and Neuchâtel (nominal 3%).
claims.reviewer_notes: Three descendant-taxing cantons + thresholds confirmed 2026-06-16.

claims.claim_text: Foreign trusts may be recognized under the Hague Trust Convention, but mandatory succession, matrimonial-property and other rules can limit outcomes; no domestic Swiss trust yet.
claims.reviewer_notes: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: art.15 mandatory-succession override is a legal conclusion needing primary treaty text; reworded.

claims.claim_text: Lump-sum (expenditure-based) taxation available to qualifying foreigners without Swiss gainful activity; cantonal option abolished in Zurich, Schaffhausen, Appenzell Ausserrhoden, Basel-Landschaft, Basel-Stadt.
claims.reviewer_notes: Cantonal abolition list confirmed 2026-06-16.
